words.definition_en: to stop something from moving through or along something else; a large piece of a solid substance that has flat sides
words.definition_vi: chặn, ngăn chặn; khối, tảng (vật liệu)
words.example: A huge rock blocked the road. / The building is made of concrete blocks.
words.example_vi: Một tảng đá khổng lồ đã chặn con đường. / Tòa nhà được xây từ các khối bê tông.

words.definition_en: hard but easily broken
words.example: As you get older your bones become increasingly brittle.
words.example_vi: Khi bạn già đi, xương của bạn ngày càng trở nên giòn (dễ gãy).

words.definition_en: to break a small piece off something; a small piece of something that has been broken off
words.definition_vi: làm mẻ, bị mẻ; mảnh mẻ, mảnh vỡ nhỏ
words.example: I've chipped a tooth. / There are chips of glass on the floor.
words.example_vi: Tôi bị sứt một chiếc răng. / Có những mảnh kính vỡ trên sàn nhà.

words.definition_en: to press something together so that it becomes smaller or more solid; smaller than is usual for something of the same kind
words.definition_vi: nén, làm cho đặc lại; nhỏ gọn, cô đọng
words.example: The waste is compacted into blocks. / The device is very compact and portable.
words.example_vi: Rác thải được nén thành các khối. / Thiết bị này rất nhỏ gọn và dễ mang theo.

words.definition_en: to make a liquid stronger by removing water from it
words.definition_vi: cô đặc, làm cho đậm đặc
words.example: The solution is concentrated by boiling it.
words.example_vi: Bạn thực sự phải tập trung cao độ khi viết thư pháp.

words.definition_en: to damage something so that a line or long narrow hole appears on its surface, but it does not break into pieces; a line on a surface where something is beginning to break
words.definition_vi: làm nứt, bị nứt; vết nứt
words.example: The window cracked when the ball hit it. / There's a crack in the wall.
words.example_vi: Cửa sổ đã bị nứt khi quả bóng đập trúng. / Có một vết nứt trên tường.

words.definition_en: a very small piece of bread, cake, or biscuit
words.example: She brushed the crumbs off the table.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy gạt những mẩu vụn khỏi bàn.

words.definition_en: to press something so hard that it is damaged or its shape is changed; a crowd of people pressed together
words.definition_vi: nghiền nát, đè nát; sự chen lấn
words.example: The car was crushed in the accident.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe đã bị nghiền nát trong vụ tai nạn.

words.definition_en: containing a lot of people or things close together; thick
words.definition_vi: dày đặc, đông đúc, có mật độ cao
words.example: The city has a very dense population.
words.example_vi: Thành phố này có mật độ dân cư rất dày đặc.

words.definition_en: to make a liquid weaker by adding water or another liquid to it; (of a liquid) made weaker by adding water
words.example: Dilute the juice with water before drinking it.
words.example_vi: Hãy pha loãng nước trái cây với nước trước khi uống.

words.definition_en: to mix with a liquid and become part of it
words.example: Salt dissolves in water.
words.example_vi: Muối tan trong nước.

words.definition_en: cloth used for making clothes, curtains, etc.
words.example: This fabric is very soft to the touch.
words.example_vi: Loại vải này sờ vào rất mềm mại.

words.definition_en: not soft, but not completely hard
words.example: The mattress is nice and firm.
words.example_vi: Tấm nệm này chắc chắn và rất thích.

words.definition_en: to come off in small, thin pieces; a small, thin piece of something
words.definition_vi: bong ra từng mảng; mảng, lớp mỏng
words.example: The paint is beginning to flake off. / Snow flakes fell from the sky.
words.example_vi: Sơn đang bắt đầu bong tróc. / Những bông tuyết rơi xuống từ bầu trời.

words.definition_en: easily broken or damaged
words.example: Be careful with that vase, it's very fragile.
words.example_vi: Hãy cẩn thận với chiếc bình đó, nó rất dễ vỡ.

words.definition_en: the force that makes it difficult for one object to slide along the surface of another
words.example: Friction between the tires and the road creates heat.
words.example_vi: Ma sát giữa lốp xe và mặt đường tạo ra nhiệt.

words.definition_en: a very small individual piece of a substance such as sand, salt, or sugar
words.example: There were grains of sand in my shoes.
words.example_vi: Có những hạt cát trong giày của tôi.

words.definition_en: the force that attracts objects towards one another, especially the force that makes things fall to the ground
words.example: Newton discovered the law of gravity.
words.example_vi: Newton đã tìm ra định luật vạn vật hấp dẫn.

words.definition_en: to break something into very small pieces or powder by rubbing it between two hard surfaces
words.example: I like to grind my own coffee beans.
words.example_vi: Tôi thích tự xay hạt cà phê của mình.

words.definition_en: having an empty space inside
words.example: Bamboo is hollow inside.
words.example_vi: Tre có ruột rỗng bên trong.

words.definition_en: a substance that is not a solid or a gas, for example water or milk
words.example: Mercury is a liquid metal at room temperature.
words.example_vi: Thủy ngân là một kim loại lỏng ở nhiệt độ phòng.

words.definition_en: a piece of something hard or solid, usually without a particular shape
words.definition_vi: cục, tảng (không hình dạng nhất định)
words.example: He put two lumps of sugar in his coffee.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã bỏ hai viên đường vào cà phê.

words.definition_en: a natural substance such as coal, salt, oil, or gold that is found in the ground
words.example: The area is rich in minerals.
words.example_vi: Khu vực này giàu khoáng sản.

words.definition_en: to shape a soft substance by pressing it into a container; a container that you pour a liquid or soft substance into so that it becomes solid in the same shape
words.example: The children were moulding clay into animal shapes.
words.example_vi: Lũ trẻ đang nặn đất sét thành hình các con vật.

words.definition_en: not allowing light to pass through; difficult to understand
words.definition_vi: đục, không trong suốt; khó hiểu
words.example: The windows were painted with an opaque white film.
words.example_vi: Cửa sổ được sơn một lớp màng trắng đục.

words.definition_en: to touch someone or something gently and repeatedly with the flat part of your hand
words.example: He patted the dog on the head.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy xoa đầu chú chó.

words.definition_en: a number of things put on top of each other; to put things one on top of another
words.example: There was a pile of books on the floor.
words.example_vi: Có một chồng sách trên sàn nhà.

words.definition_en: to rub something in order to make it shiny; a substance used for making something shiny
words.example: I need to polish my shoes before the wedding.
words.example_vi: Tôi cần đánh bóng giày trước khi đi dự đám cưới.

words.definition_en: to pull your nails along your skin, especially because you have an itch; a thin mark on a surface
words.example: Don't scratch your mosquito bites. / There's a scratch on the car door.
words.example_vi: Đừng gãi các vết muỗi đốt. / Có một vết xước trên cửa xe hơi.

words.definition_en: to rub something hard, especially with a stiff brush, in order to clean it
words.example: I had to scrub the floor to get it clean.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã phải cọ rửa sàn nhà để làm sạch nó.

words.definition_en: to break into many pieces, especially by hitting or dropping something
words.definition_vi: đập tan, vỡ tan thành mảnh vụn
words.example: The plate smashed when it hit the floor.
words.example_vi: Chiếc đĩa đã vỡ tan tành khi rơi xuống sàn.

words.definition_en: a substance that keeps its shape; hard and of a single substance throughout
words.example: Ice is solid water. / The wall is made of solid oak.
words.example_vi: Băng là nước ở thể rắn. / Bức tường được làm từ gỗ sồi nguyên khối.

words.definition_en: a very small spot or mark
words.example: There's not a speck of dust in the room.
words.example_vi: Không có một hạt bụi nào trong phòng.

words.definition_en: to damage something by pressing it flat; to push yourself into a space that is too small
words.definition_vi: đè bẹp, ép; sự chen chúc
words.example: The box was squashed in the mail.
words.example_vi: Chiếc hộp đã bị bẹp rúm trong quá trình vận chuyển qua bưu điện.

words.definition_en: to press something firmly, especially with your hands
words.example: Squeeze the lemon to get the juice out.
words.example_vi: Vắt chanh để lấy nước cốt.

words.definition_en: a pile of things arranged one on top of another; to arrange things in a stack
words.definition_vi: chồng, đống (xếp ngăn nắp); xếp thành chồng
words.example: He stacked the boxes in the corner.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã xếp chồng những chiếc hộp vào trong góc.

words.definition_en: firm and difficult to bend or move
words.example: The new shoes are a bit stiff.
words.example_vi: Đôi giày mới hơi bị cứng.

words.definition_en: to move your hand gently over something
words.example: She stroked the cat's fur.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy vuốt ve bộ lông của con mèo.

words.definition_en: to push something quickly and carelessly into a space; a variety of objects or substances
words.definition_vi: nhồi nhét; đồ đạc, vật liệu
words.example: I stuffed my clothes into the suitcase.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã nhồi nhét quần áo vào va li.

words.definition_en: a particular type of liquid, solid, or gas
words.example: The police found a strange substance in the car.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã tìm thấy một chất lạ trong xe hơi.

words.definition_en: made by combining different substances rather than being natural
words.example: Synthetic fabrics like nylon are very durable.
words.example_vi: Các loại vải tổng hợp như nylon rất bền.

words.definition_en: to pull something so that it separates into two pieces or a hole is created in it
words.example: I tore the letter in half.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã xé lá thư làm đôi.

words.definition_en: the way that something feels when you touch it
words.definition_vi: kết cấu, bề mặt (khi chạm vào)
words.example: The fabric has a smooth texture.
words.example_vi: Loại vải này có kết cấu bề mặt mịn màng.

words.definition_en: allowing light to pass through so that you can see through it clearly
words.example: Glass is transparent.
words.example_vi: Thủy tinh trong suốt.

words.definition_en: having a lot of buildings close together
words.definition_vi: có mật độ xây dựng cao
words.example: This is a very built-up area with no green spaces.
words.example_vi: Đây là một khu vực có mật độ xây dựng dày đặc và không có không gian xanh.

words.definition_en: to avoid something by going around it; a road that goes around a town or city
words.example: We can bypass the city center by taking the new road.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta có thể đi đường vòng tránh trung tâm thành phố bằng cách đi con đường mới.

words.definition_en: to build something large such as a house, bridge, or road
words.example: The bridge was constructed in the 19th century.
words.example_vi: Cây cầu được xây dựng vào thế kỷ 19.

words.definition_en: to deliberately destroy a building
words.example: The old factory was demolished to make way for a shopping mall.
words.example_vi: Nhà máy cũ đã bị dỡ bỏ để nhường chỗ cho một trung tâm mua sắm.

words.definition_en: a particular area of a city or country
words.example: The business district is very busy during the day.
words.example_vi: Khu vực kinh doanh rất nhộn nhịp vào ban ngày.

words.definition_en: to live in a particular place
words.example: They dwell in the forest.
words.example_vi: Họ cư ngụ trong rừng.

words.definition_en: an area where there are many houses or factories built at the same time
words.definition_vi: khu dân cư, khu công nghiệp
words.example: She lives on a large housing estate.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy sống trong một khu dân cư lớn.

words.definition_en: to legally force someone to leave the house they are living in
words.example: The family was evicted for not paying the rent.
words.example_vi: Gia đình đã bị trục xuất vì không trả tiền thuê nhà.

words.definition_en: a very tall building with many floors
words.example: Many high-rise blocks were built in the 1960s.
words.example_vi: Nhiều tòa nhà cao tầng đã được xây dựng vào những năm 1960.

words.definition_en: buildings for people to live in
words.example: The city needs more affordable housing.
words.example_vi: Thành phố cần thêm nhiều nhà ở giá rẻ.

words.definition_en: the basic systems and services that a country or organization uses in order to work effectively
words.example: The country's infrastructure needs urgent investment.
words.example_vi: Cơ sở hạ tầng của đất nước cần được đầu tư khẩn cấp.

words.definition_en: the part of a city near the center, often where there are social problems
words.definition_vi: khu vực nội thành (thường là khu cũ, nghèo)
words.example: Inner city areas often suffer from poverty.
words.example_vi: Các khu vực nội đô thường phải chịu cảnh nghèo đói.

words.definition_en: to live or work in a room or building
words.example: The company occupies the top three floors of the building.
words.example_vi: Công ty chiếm giữ ba tầng trên cùng của tòa nhà.

words.definition_en: having people living there
words.example: The valley is sparsely populated.
words.example_vi: Thung lũng này có dân cư thưa thớt.

words.definition_en: the shape made by buildings or mountains against the sky
words.example: The New York skyline is iconic.
words.example_vi: Đường chân trời của New York mang tính biểu tượng.

words.definition_en: a very tall modern building
words.example: Skyscrapers dominate the city center.
words.example_vi: Các tòa nhà chọc trời chiếm ưu thế ở trung tâm thành phố.

words.definition_en: something that has been built
words.example: The cathedral is a magnificent structure.
words.example_vi: Nhà thờ lớn là một công trình kiến trúc tráng lệ.

words.definition_en: relating to an area where people live that is outside the center of a city
words.example: They live in a quiet suburban street.
words.example_vi: Họ sống ở một con phố ngoại ô yên tĩnh.

words.definition_en: the objects and conditions that are around a person or thing
words.example: The house is set in beautiful surroundings.
words.example_vi: Ngôi nhà nằm trong một khung cảnh xung quanh tuyệt đẹp.

words.definition_en: relating to towns and cities
words.example: Pollution is a major problem in urban areas.
words.example_vi: Ô nhiễm là một vấn đề lớn ở các khu vực đô thị.

words.definition_en: cover a window or door with wooden boards
words.example: The shop was boarded up after it closed down.
words.example_vi: Cửa hàng đã được đóng ván kín mít sau khi nó ngừng hoạt động.

words.definition_en: lock the doors of a building or business
words.definition_vi: đóng cửa, khóa cửa (tòa nhà, doanh nghiệp)
words.example: The manager closed up the shop at 6 PM.
words.example_vi: Người quản lý đã đóng cửa tiệm lúc 6 giờ tối.

words.definition_en: be removed from something such as clothing or cloth by washing or rubbing; have a particular result or end in a particular way
words.definition_vi: tẩy sạch (vết bẩn); có kết quả
words.example: The ink stain won't come out. / How did the cake come out?
words.example_vi: Vết mực này không sạch được. / Chiếc bánh ra lò trông thế nào?

words.definition_en: remove something from a larger piece by cutting; stop eating something or doing something, especially because it is bad for your health
words.definition_vi: cắt ra; cắt giảm, ngừng (thói quen xấu)
words.example: Cut out the pictures and paste them here. / I've cut out sugar from my diet.
words.example_vi: Cắt các bức hình ra rồi dán chúng vào đây. / Tôi đã cắt giảm đường khỏi chế độ ăn uống của mình.

words.definition_en: clean, repair or decorate something
words.example: We're going to fix up the old house before moving in.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi định sửa sang lại ngôi nhà cũ trước khi dọn vào ở.

words.definition_en: destroy a building or wall
words.example: They are going to knock down the old library.
words.example_vi: Họ chuẩn bị dỡ bỏ thư viện cũ.

words.definition_en: if something piles up or someone piles it up, the amount of it increases a lot
words.example: The work is really beginning to pile up.
words.example_vi: Công việc thực sự đang bắt đầu dồn đống lại.

words.definition_en: stop something from falling by putting something under it or against it; help a government, system, organisation, etc continue to exist, especially by providing financial or military support
words.definition_vi: chống đỡ; hỗ trợ (tài chính, quân sự) để duy trì
words.example: The wall was propped up with wooden beams.
words.example_vi: Bức tường được chống đỡ bằng những thanh dầm gỗ.

words.definition_en: fix something such as equipment in the place where it will be used and make it ready to use; make an official request, claim, offer, etc
words.definition_vi: lắp đặt; đưa ra (yêu cầu, đề nghị)
words.example: We're having a new shower put in.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã dành rất nhiều công sức cho dự án này.

words.definition_en: make something by joining all its parts; produce or organize something using many different things; choose people or things to form a team or group
words.definition_vi: lắp ráp; tổ chức, kết hợp
words.example: It took hours to put the IKEA desk together.
words.example_vi: Mất hàng giờ đồng hồ để lắp ráp chiếc bàn IKEA này.

words.definition_en: build something such as a wall, fence or house; let someone stay in your house
words.definition_vi: xây dựng (tường, hàng rào); cho ai ở nhờ
words.example: They've put up a new fence. / Can you put me up for the night?
words.example_vi: Họ vừa dựng một hàng rào mới. / Bạn có thể cho tôi ngủ nhờ một đêm được không?

words.definition_en: build a structure or put it in a particular place; make a piece of equipment ready for use
words.definition_vi: dựng lên; thiết lập, chuẩn bị sử dụng
words.example: We set up the tent in the backyard.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã dựng lều ở sân sau.

words.definition_en: if people in a group spread out, they move away from one another so that they cover a large area
words.example: The search party spread out across the field.
words.example_vi: Đội tìm kiếm đã tỏa ra khắp cánh đồng.

words.definition_en: separate a large structure into pieces; write down information or a statement
words.example: The scaffolding was taken down after the work was finished.
words.example_vi: Giàn giáo đã được tháo dỡ sau khi công việc hoàn thành.

words.definition_en: add water to a drink or liquid in order to make it less strong, dilute; make something such as a statement or newspaper article less offensive, powerful or detailed
words.definition_vi: pha loãng; làm cho nhẹ bớt, giảm sức thuyết phục/chi tiết
words.example: The soup was watered down. / The report was watered down before publication.
words.example_vi: Món súp đã bị pha loãng. / Bản báo cáo đã bị nói giảm nói tránh trước khi công bố.

words.definition_en: make something gradually disappear or become thinner by using or rubbing it; make someone gradually lose their energy or confidence
words.definition_vi: mòn đi; làm cho ai đó kiệt sức/mất tự tin
words.example: The steps had been worn down by millions of feet.
words.example_vi: Các bậc thang đã bị mòn đi bởi hàng triệu bước chân.

words.definition_en: a place where you feel as relaxed as you do in your own home
words.definition_vi: nơi thoải mái như ở nhà mình
words.example: The hotel was a real home from home.
words.example_vi: Khách sạn này thực sự tạo cảm giác thoải mái như ở nhà.

words.definition_en: something that makes a place look less attractive, for example an ugly building
words.definition_vi: thứ làm mất vẻ đẹp cảnh quan
words.example: That new factory is a real blot on the landscape.
words.example_vi: Nhà máy mới đó thực sự là một cảnh tượng chướng mắt làm hỏng cả mỹ quan.

words.definition_en: area surrounding a large city where many people who work in that city live
words.definition_vi: vùng ngoại ô dành cho người đi làm trong thành phố
words.example: Many families prefer living in the commuter belt.
words.example_vi: Nhiều gia đình thích sống ở vùng ven dành cho người đi làm trong thành phố.

words.definition_en: unpleasant part of a city where there are a lot of ugly buildings close together
words.definition_vi: khu rừng bê tông (nơi thành phố ngột ngạt)
words.example: I hate living in this concrete jungle.
words.example_vi: Tôi ghét sống trong cái "khu rừng bê tông" này.

words.definition_en: used for saying that you are happy to be back in your own home
words.example: After a long trip, it's home sweet home.
words.example_vi: Sau một chuyến đi dài, không đâu bằng nhà mình.

words.definition_en: in an area far from the town or city
words.definition_vi: ở vùng hẻo lánh, vùng sâu vùng xa
words.example: They live out in the sticks, miles from anywhere.
words.example_vi: Họ sống ở nơi khỉ ho cò gáy, cách xa mọi thứ hàng dặm.

words.definition_en: feel relaxed and behave in the same way as you do in your own home
words.definition_vi: cứ tự nhiên như ở nhà
words.example: Come in and make yourself at home.
words.example_vi: Mời vào, cứ tự nhiên như ở nhà nhé.

words.definition_en: with no place to live
words.definition_vi: sống lang thang trên đường phố
words.example: Many young people end up on the street.
words.example_vi: Nhiều người trẻ cuối cùng lâm vào cảnh vô gia cư.

words.definition_en: spending the evening enjoying yourself in bars, clubs, theatres, etc
words.example: We're going out on the town tonight.
words.example_vi: Tối nay chúng ta sẽ đi chơi đêm ở trung tâm thành phố.

words.definition_en: town or city that is close to and depends on a larger city
words.example: The town developed as a satellite town of London.
words.example_vi: Thị trấn này đã phát triển như một đô thị vệ tinh của London.

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes.

items.explanation: A 'pile of clothes' refers to a number of clothes put on top of each other.
items.sentence: When are you going to move that ___ of clothes in your bedroom?

items.explanation: A 'lump of coal' is a solid piece of coal without a specific shape.
items.sentence: The old man slowly put a few more ___ of coal on the dying fire.

items.explanation: Paint comes off in small, thin pieces called 'flakes'.
items.sentence: We need to redecorate. Look - there are ___ of paint coming off the walls.

items.explanation: Concrete is often produced in large pieces with flat sides called 'blocks'.
items.sentence: The building is composed of ___ of concrete held together by metal poles.

items.explanation: A 'speck of dust' is a very small spot or particle.
items.sentence: When I come back, I don't want to find a single ___ of dust in here.

items.explanation: Sand consists of very small individual particles called 'grains'.
items.sentence: How many ___ of sand do you think there are on this beach?

items.explanation: Small pieces of bread or sandwiches are called 'crumbs'.
items.sentence: Eat your sandwich in the kitchen, because I don't want ___ on my new sofa.

items.explanation: Small pieces broken off glass are called 'chips'.
items.sentence: Be careful! I dropped something and there are ___ of glass all over the floor.

items.explanation: To 'scratch' is to pull nails along the skin to relieve an itch.
items.sentence: Don't ___ your mosquito bites or you'll make them worse.

items.explanation: To 'pat' someone on the back is to touch them gently with a flat hand.
items.sentence: I felt great when the coach ___ me on the back and said, 'Well done'.

items.explanation: To 'polish' shoes is to rub them to make them shiny.
items.sentence: Martin quickly ___ his shoes and walked out the door.

items.explanation: To 'stroke' fur is to move your hand gently over it.
items.sentence: Don't ___ the dog's fur the wrong way. She doesn't like it.

items.explanation: To 'scrub' is to rub hard to clean off something like graffiti.
items.sentence: I had to really ___ that wall to get the graffiti off it.

items.explanation: To 'squeeze' a T-shirt is to press it to remove water.
items.sentence: You'll have to ___ your T-shirt to get all the water out of it.

items.explanation: To 'crush' ice is to break it into small pieces.
items.sentence: You get the orange juice, and I'll ___ some ice.

items.explanation: Coffee beans are 'ground' (verb: grind) into powder.
items.sentence: I like to ___ my own coffee because it tastes so much better.

items.explanation: To 'squash' a spider is to crush it flat.
items.sentence: Don't ___ that spider! Just put it outside on the grass.

items.explanation: To 'fold' paper is to bend it so one part covers another.
items.sentence: ___ the paper in half and give half to the student next to you.

items.explanation: A 'crack' is a line on a surface that isn't fully broken.
items.sentence: You haven't broken the window, but you have ___ it.

items.explanation: To 'smash' is to break something violently into many pieces.
items.sentence: Eva was so angry she ___ a glass against the wall.

items.explanation: Something 'fragile' is easily damaged or broken.
items.sentence: Be careful with that old wedding dress - it's very ___ .

items.explanation: Glass is 'transparent' because you can see through it.
items.sentence: Why can you see through glass, whereas concrete isn't ___ at all?

items.explanation: Paper that is 'stiff' is firm and difficult to bend.
items.sentence: This paper isn't ___ enough to make a birthday card out of.

items.explanation: 'Opaque' means not allowing light to pass through; opposite of transparent.
items.sentence: The windows of the limousine were ___ , so I couldn't see who was inside.

items.explanation: 'Brittle' materials are hard but break easily after exposure to sun/heat.
items.sentence: After being out in the sun for a month, the plastic container became ___ .

items.explanation: Rocks are 'dense' (thick/heavy), which makes them sink.
items.sentence: Rocks sink in water because they are ___ .

items.explanation: A 'hollow' object has empty space inside and sounds different when knocked.
items.sentence: This wall appears solid, but when you knock on it, it sounds ___ .

items.explanation: To 'stuff' is to push things quickly into a space like a suitcase.
items.sentence: Alice quickly ___ a few clothes into a suitcase and ran out of the house.

items.explanation: To 'stack' is to pile things neatly on top of each other.
items.sentence: You could help by ___ those boxes over there.

items.explanation: To 'mould' clay is to shape it into an object.
items.sentence: Try to ___ the clay into the basic shape of a vase.

exercises.label: A

exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in bold in the correct form.

items.explanation: Friction stops objects moving; solids are hard objects; ice has a thin liquid layer; gravity makes you fall.
items.sentence: Why do we slip on ice, but not on wood? The answer is ___, the force that stops one object moving against another. When two ___ are in contact, tiny bumps on each surface prevent them from moving easily. The surface of ice is actually covered in ___, so your shoes slide over it. Once you slip, the force of ___ takes over, and you fall down!

items.explanation: Nylon is synthetic; fibers make fabric; texture is how it feels.
items.sentence: Nylon was the first ___ material to be produced using coal, water and air. Since its invention in 1935, it has been used to make many products and long thin strands of it can be made into ___ for clothing. Although cheap, many people dislike the plastic ___ of nylon and prefer natural fibres, such as cotton.

items.explanation: Pressure compacts rocks making them firm; a pure substance in rock form is a mineral.
items.sentence: Rocks are formed in different ways. One type, metamorphic rocks, are produced by high pressure beneath the Earth's surface, which ___ the rocks and makes them ___ . Most rocks are a mixture of different chemicals. When a ___ occurs in rock form but is not mixed with other chemicals, it is known as a ___ .

items.explanation: Salt dissolves in water; more water makes it dilute; more salt makes it concentrate.
items.sentence: If you take a glass of water and ___ salt in it, you produce what's called a solution. If you add more water, the solution becomes more ___ . You can ___ the solution by adding more salt.

exercises.label: B

exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.explanation: A bypass is a road that goes around a city to avoid traffic.
items.sentence: The proposed (1) ___ around the city of Rushton has run into more controversy.

items.explanation: To construct a road means to build it.
items.sentence: The local council have been attempting to (2) ___ the road for five years now...

items.explanation: The inner city is the central part of a city where traffic is heavy.
items.sentence: ...in order to ease traffic in the (3) ___ ,

items.explanation: Densely populated means many people live in the area.
items.sentence: ...which is amongst the most densely (4) ___ areas in the country.

items.explanation: Suburban areas are residential districts on the outskirts of a city.
items.sentence: However, residents in (5) ___ areas, on the outskirts of the city, are up in arms...

items.explanation: Surroundings refer to the environment around a house.
items.sentence: ...about the plan, which they claim would spoil the (6) ___ .

items.explanation: A house occupies a position or space.
items.sentence: 'We live in beautiful (7) ___ here,' commented Ada Fowler, one of those whose house (8) ___ a position next to the proposed route...

items.explanation: Urban planning is the technical and political process concerned with the use of land and design of the urban environment.
items.sentence: 'The council have no real concept of (9) ___ planning.'

items.explanation: To dwell means to live in a certain place.
items.sentence: Many of those who (10) ___ in the area agree.

exercises.label: C

items.explanation: A built-up area has many buildings.
items.sentence: I'd hate to live in such a ___ area as this.

items.explanation: A high-rise block is a tall building with many floors.
items.sentence: My grandparents have just moved into a ___ block and they seem to like it.

items.explanation: A skyscraper is an exceptionally tall building.
items.sentence: The first ___ was made possible by advances in the use of metal and glass...

items.explanation: The skyline is the outline of buildings against the sky.
items.sentence: I love to look at the ___ of London at night, don't you?

items.explanation: Housing refers to buildings for living in provided by the government.
items.sentence: The government is planning to provide low-cost ___ for people on low incomes.

items.explanation: A local estate is a housing development area.
items.sentence: I grew up on a local ___ , so I know the area quite well.

items.explanation: To demolish a building is to destroy it.
items.sentence: They're going to ___ the old Town Hall today.

items.explanation: To evict is to force someone out of their home legally.
items.sentence: You can't ___ a poor old woman like that and just leave her homeless!

items.explanation: A structure is a building or construction.
items.sentence: St Paul's Cathedral is a really impressive ___ .

items.explanation: Infrastructure refers to the basic systems like transport and communications.
items.sentence: This country just doesn't have the ___ to host the Olympic Games.

exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.explanation: boarded up: covered with boards.
items.sentence: ...it's not unusual to see buildings which have been boarded (1) ___ .

items.explanation: closed up: locked and finished for the day.
items.sentence: After businesses have closed (2) ___ for the day...

items.explanation: piling up: increasing in amount.
items.sentence: The problems of decay have been piling (3) ___ for years...

items.explanation: fix up: repair and decorate.
items.sentence: ...councils are finally deciding to fix inner city areas (4) ___ to attract residents...

items.explanation: knocking down: destroying/demolishing.
items.sentence: The process begins by knocking (5) ___ old, ugly buildings...

items.explanation: putting up: building.
items.sentence: ...and putting (6) ___ attractive office and apartment blocks.

items.explanation: taken down: removed from a position.
items.sentence: Advertising hoardings are taken (7) ___...

items.explanation: put in: installed or established.
items.sentence: ...and facilities... are put (8) ___ .

exercises.label: E

exercises.instruction: Complete using a phrasal verb with a word from box A in the right form and a word from box B. You need to use some words from box B more than once.

items.explanation: Set up means to build or assemble a structure like a tent.
items.sentence: The fair must be happening in town because they've ___ tents.

items.explanation: Cut out means to remove a shape from a larger piece of material.
items.sentence: ___ a circle ___ of the first piece of card and stick it on the second piece.

items.explanation: Water down means to dilute a liquid.
items.sentence: If the juice is too strong for you, why don't you ___ it ___ ?

items.explanation: Prop up means to support something to stop it from falling.
items.sentence: That wall would fall over if it wasn't ___ with planks of wood.

items.explanation: Come out means to be removed (as in a stain).
items.sentence: You've got blood on your collar. It won't ___ , you know.

items.explanation: Spread out means to cover a large area by moving away from each other.
items.sentence: I've got a large family, but they've all ___ across the whole country.

items.explanation: Put together means to assemble parts.
items.sentence: I hate buying furniture from that place because you have to ___ it ___ yourself.

items.explanation: Wear down means to become thinner due to use.
items.sentence: The steps were ___ after many years of use.

exercises.label: F

exercises.instruction: The phrasal verb pile up has a meaning connected to 'increase'. Tick the sentences where the phrasal verb means something like 'increase'.

items.explanation: put up = increase (prices)
items.sentence: I see the supermarket has put its prices up again .

items.explanation: speed up = increase (speed)
items.sentence: Speed up, or we're never going to get there!

items.explanation: wipe up = clean a spill
items.sentence: Pass me a cloth and I'll just wipe up that water on the table .

items.explanation: mount up = increase in amount
items.sentence: Work is really beginning to mount up, so I'll have to go into the office this weekend .

items.explanation: cough up = pay/produce something unwillingly
items.sentence: Marianne had something stuck in her throat, but she finally coughed it up .

items.explanation: speak up = increase (volume)
items.sentence: You'll have to speak up because I'm a little deaf, you know .

items.explanation: build up = increase (strength)
items.sentence: You need to build up your strength if you're going to take part in the marathon .

items.explanation: turn up = increase (volume)
items.sentence: Oh, I wanted to watch this programme. Turn the TV up .

items.explanation: made up = reconciled after an argument
items.sentence: Jill and I had a terrible row, but we soon made up .

items.explanation: fatten up = increase (weight)
items.sentence: Honestly, I couldn't eat another thing! What are you trying to do - fatten me up?

exercises.label: G

exercises.instruction: Complete using the phrases in the box.

items.explanation: Smooth sailing means a process without problems.
items.sentence: We had a few difficulties at first, but it was pretty ___ after that.

items.explanation: On the house means provided for free by the establishment.
items.sentence: Don't worry - these drinks are ___ .

items.explanation: Raw materials are basic substances used to create something.
items.sentence: Lance built a log cabin in a Canadian forest, using ___ that were all around him.

items.explanation: Collocation: the town of [Name].
items.sentence: Isambard Kingdom Brunel, the great engineer, was born in ___ Portsmouth in 1806.

items.explanation: On the table means being offered for consideration.
items.sentence: The offer ___ is far too low and I'm going to ask them to increase it.

items.explanation: Bricks and mortar refers to physical property/investment.
items.sentence: I would invest in ___ , because property is usually a safe investment.

items.explanation: Out of shape means not physically fit.
items.sentence: Charles was so ___ that he was exhausted after just half a day on the building site.

items.explanation: Precious metals are rare metals like gold or silver.
items.sentence: A ___ such as silver is valuable because of its rarity.

items.explanation: bend something into shape.

items.explanation: erect a monument to someone.

items.explanation: have no foundation: not based on facts.

exercises.label: I

exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.explanation: without foundation (no proof); foundation stone (first stone of building); foundation course (prep course).
items.sentence: These accusations are completely without ___ . / The ___ stone for the new hospital was laid by the Mayor. / You have to do a ___ course before you can start your degree.

items.explanation: tough guy (strong/aggressive); get tough with (strict); tough on Amanda (difficult for).
items.sentence: Joshua looks aggressive, but he's not such a ___ guy once you get to know him. / It's time this government got ___ with people exceeding the speed limit. / The last few years have been quite ___ on Amanda...

items.explanation: a matter of months (short time); no matter what (regardless); a matter of urgency (important).
items.sentence: The building collapsed only a ___ of months after construction was completed. / ...this month, no ___ what. / ...as a ___ of some urgency.

items.explanation: take the floor (start speaking); ground floor (level); floor show (entertainment).
items.sentence: There was a hush in Parliament as the Minister took the ___ . / I'd be afraid of burglars if I lived in a ground ___ flat. / ...I thought the ___ show was terrible.

items.explanation: quick off the mark (fast to react); hit the mark (correct); leave a mark (lasting effect).
items.sentence: The Smiths were pretty quick off the ___ to sell... / ...really hit the ___ . / ...have certainly left a ___ on this area.

items.explanation: businesses fold (fail/close); fold paper (bend); fold arms (cross arms).
items.sentence: A record number of businesses are predicted to ___ in the next 12 months. / Now carefully ___ the paper in two... / Don't ___ your arms and talk to me in that tone of voice!

items.explanation: home sweet home: happy to be home.

exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold is in the wrong form. Rewrite them correctly.

items.explanation: Manufacturing industries (industry of making products).

items.explanation: Surrounding areas (areas nearby).
items.sentence: Dunscar and the roundness (surrounding) areas have seen a sharp increase in house prices .

items.explanation: To solidify (become solid).
items.sentence: When the mixture starts to solidity (solidify), put it in the freezer .

items.explanation: A household (people living in a house).
items.sentence: Each housing (household) is responsible for maintaining the area in front of their house .

items.explanation: Adjective: substantial (large/significant).

items.explanation: Structural damage (damage to the building's structure).

items.explanation: Noun: expressions of disagreement.

items.explanation: Adverb: with a high density.

items.explanation: Adjective: relating to architecture.

items.sentence: black
bright
broad
brown
damp
deep
fright
hard
length
loose
narrow
red
short
strength
tall
tight
weak

exercises.label: N

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using words formed in exercise N in the correct form.

items.explanation: Widen means to make wider.
items.sentence: Apparently, they're going to ___ the road here so that it can cope with the heavy traffic.

items.explanation: Strengthen means to make stronger.
items.sentence: This decision by the authorities only ___ our determination to fight on.

items.explanation: Blacken means to become black.
items.sentence: Ryan's face ___ as he realised what a terrible mistake he'd made.

items.explanation: Shorten means to make shorter.
items.sentence: The dress suits you, but it's a bit long. You can always ___ it.

items.explanation: Loosen means to make less tight.
items.sentence: Could you help me ___ my seat belt? It's a bit loose.

items.explanation: Brighten up means to make a place more cheerful.
items.sentence: Some flowers in here would certainly ___ the place up, wouldn't they?

items.explanation: Harden means to become hard.
items.sentence: They leave the clay in the sun to ___ and use the bricks to build houses.

items.explanation: Weaken means to become less strong.
items.sentence: We must remain strong and not ___ if we're going to stop this bypass being built.

exercises.label: O
